title: Contributing to envar
text: Fork package clone onto computer. haven’t done , recommend using usethis::create_from_github("animalbiodiversitylab/envar", fork = TRUE). Install development dependencies devtools::install_dev_deps(), make sure package passes R CMD check running devtools::check(). R CMD check doesn’t pass cleanly, ’s good idea ask help continuing. Create Git branch pull request (PR). recommend using usethis::pr_init("brief-description--change"). Make changes, commit git, create PR running usethis::pr_push(), following prompts browser. title PR briefly describe change. body PR contain Fixes #issue-number. user-facing changes, add bullet top NEWS.md (.e. just first header). title: Get started
text: , show full working example use package download process set environmental variables specific study area. download set climatic, topographic, land cover variables study area European Alps (already included package object Alps()). call var_get() function used define study area (argument “shape”), desired grid output resolution km (argument “res”), coordinate reference system (argument “crs”), eventual buffer (km) expand study area (argument “buffer”). default resolution always 30 arcseconds (0.008333333°, ~ 1 km) equator, “res” specifies factor aggregate (default 1 doesn’t apply change). , “%>%” used concatenate different commands. next step set specific functions used download process variables different sources (see reference full list). call generic source structured : sourcename(vars = c()), sourcename name function devoted one source inside “vars” argument specific variables downloaded source specified. example, first download set 5 bioclimatic variables CHELSA source (Karger et al. 2017), download elevation slope topography source (Amatulli et al. 2018), finally download percentage cover trees ice 1 km resolution global land cover based ESA 30 m land cover (Lo Parrino et al. 2025). correlation analysis can performed corr_check() function identify remove highly correlated variables. function must always called last one pipeline.
code: processed <- var_get(shape = Alps, crs = 3035, res = 2, buffer = 10) %>%               chelsa(vars = c("bio1", "bio4", "bio10", "bio12", "bio19"),                     years = "1981-2010") %>%              topography(vars = c("elevation", "slope")) %>%              esalandcover(vars = c("trees", "ice")) %>%              corr_check()

title: Package overview
text: single layer downloaded maintains original extent. additionally projected desired Coordinate Reference System (CRS). default CRS envar Geographic Reference System (GRS) WGS 84 (EPSG: 4326). commonly used GRS global analyses. GRS assume Earth ellipsoid (spheroid) provide coordinates based 3D surface degrees latitude-longitude values. However, systems ideal compute distances meaning degrees changes based location earth (e.g., 1 degree longitude corresponds ~ 111 km equator approaches zero towards poles). Additionally, users might want improve spatial analysis accuracy, visualize data specific projection, align results data use different CRS. Thus, Projected Reference System (PRS) can used, coordinates based location 2D system. conversion GRS PRS always implies certain amount distortion, thus different PRS optimized different areas globe locally reduce distortion moving 3D 2D representation. Thus, users can use argument “crs” var_get() function specify desired CRS. show example download single variable single source, case 1 km land cover based ESA (Lo Parrino et al. 2025), projection local equal-area projection (EPSG:3035). Inside crs argument, number character representing code PRS must provided. nothing specified, 4326 assumed. crs can also specified prefix ‘ESRI’ ‘EPSG’ according system, e.g. “EPSG:4326” “ESRI:54009”.
code: processed_singlelayer_projected <- var_get(country = "Italy", crs=3035) %>%   esalandcover(vars=c("ice")) print(processed_singlelayer_projected) ## class       : SpatRaster  ## dimensions  : 1144, 991, 1  (nrow, ncol, nlyr) ## resolution  : 1000, 1000  (x, y) ## extent      : 4055312, 5046312, 1522080, 2666080  (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) ## coord. ref. : ETRS89-extended / LAEA Europe (EPSG:3035)  ## source(s)   : memory ## name        :     ice  ## min value   :  0.0000  ## max value   : 99.9728 plot(processed_singlelayer_projected)

title: Variables
text: function downloads, processes, extracts variables near-global freshwater-specific environmental variables dataset (Domisch et al. 2015). variables available 1 km resolution capture upstream catchment characteristics, including topography, land cover, soil, climate. Available variables: freshwater() function provides access 324 near-global 1-km resolution layers. can filter specific layers within collections using month argument (climatic variables) algorithm argument (topographic, soil, aggregation methods). Temperature (monthly) Use month argument (1–12) select specific months. monthly_tmin_average.nc – monthly minimum temperature average, min temp average, tmin avg, tmin monthly_tmax_average.nc – monthly maximum temperature average, max temp average, tmax avg, tmax monthly_tmin_weighted_average.nc – monthly minimum temperature weighted, min temp weighted, tmin weighted monthly_tmax_weighted_average.nc – monthly maximum temperature weighted, max temp weighted, tmax weighted Precipitation monthly_prec_sum.nc – monthly upstream precipitation sum, precipitation sum, precip sum, prec monthly_prec_weighted_sum.nc – monthly upstream precipitation weighted, precipitation weighted, precip weighted Hydroclimatic hydroclim_average+sum.nc – hydroclimatic variables average, hydroclim average, hydroclim hydroclim_weighted_average+sum.nc – hydroclimatic variables weighted, hydroclim weighted Topography elevation.nc – upstream elevation, elevation, dem slope.nc – upstream slope, slope flow_acc.nc – stream length, flow accumulation, flow Land cover landcover_minimum.nc – upstream landcover minimum, landcover min landcover_maximum.nc – upstream landcover maximum, landcover max landcover_range.nc – upstream landcover range, landcover range landcover_average.nc – upstream landcover average, landcover avg, landcover landcover_weighted_average.nc – upstream landcover weighted, landcover weighted Geology & soil geology_weighted_sum.nc – upstream geology, geology weighted, geology soil_minimum.nc – upstream soil minimum, soil min soil_maximum.nc – upstream soil maximum, soil max soil_range.nc – upstream soil range, soil range soil_average.nc – upstream soil average, soil avg, soil soil_weighted_average.nc – upstream soil weighted, soil weighted Quality control quality_control.nc – quality control, qc Using month argument month argument used monthly variables (tmin, tmax, prec). Supplying one several months filters dataset corresponding monthly bands. Using algorithm argument algorithm argument filters specific statistical bands topography, flow accumulation, soils, land cover. elevation slope layers, algorithms map band order: * min – Band 1 * max – Band 2 * range – Band 3 * avg / mn – Band 4 Flow accumulation: * length – Band 1 (stream length) * acc – Band 2 (catchment accumulation) land cover soil variables, algorithm matches text patterns filenames (e.g., “maximum”, “weighted”, “average”). multiple variables requested, algorithm applies entire request, multiple calls may required different algorithms needed.
code: processed <- var_get(shape = Alps, crs = 3035) %>%  freshwater(vars = c("elevation", "slope", "tmin"), algorithm = "mn", month=12) plot(processed[[3]])

title: Variables
text: function downloads, processes, extracts variables EarthEnv habitat heterogeneity dataset (1-km resolution) (Tuanmu & Jetz 2015). variable corresponds global Cloud-Optimized GeoTIFF (COG) representing different metrics habitat heterogeneity derived remote sensing data. Available variables: First-order statistics: cv - coefficient variation, coeff variation evenness - even range - range shannon - shannon index, shannon entropy simpson - simpson index, simpson diversity std - standard deviation, std dev Second-order statistics (Texture metrics): Contrast - contrast Correlation - correlation, corr Dissimilarity - dissimilarity Entropy - entropy, texture entropy Homogeneity - homogeneity Maximum - maximum, max Uniformity - uniformity, uniform Variance - variance, var
code: processed <- var_get(shape = Alps, crs = 3035) %>%    heterogeneity(vars = c("shannon", "cv")) plot(processed[[1]])

what: Check Variable Correlation and Multicollinearity — check
title: Check Variable Correlation and Multicollinearity — check
text: function analyzes collinearity among environmental variables using Pearson correlation Variance Inflation Factor (VIF). automatically handles data preparation (converting rasters data frames, removing NAs, dropping constant variables), generates correlation plot saved working directory, returns summary potential issues.

title: Check Variable Correlation and Multicollinearity — check
code: check(x)

title: Check Variable Correlation and Multicollinearity — check
text: x SpatRaster (terra package) data.frame containing environmental variables analyzed.

title: Check Variable Correlation and Multicollinearity — check
text: list containing four elements: stack: original input object x. correlation_table: matrix Pearson correlation coefficients. vif_table: data frame containing VIF values, sorted descending order. correlation.summary: character vector summarizing warnings variables   correlation > 0.6 VIF > 3.

title: Check Variable Correlation and Multicollinearity — check
text: function performs following steps: Converts SpatRaster inputs data frame. Removes non-numeric columns rows containing NAs. Identifies removes variables zero variance (constant values). Calculates Pearson correlations saves plot "Corr_plot.png" current working directory. Calculates VIF using usdm::vif.

title: Check Variable Correlation and Multicollinearity — check
text: correlation plot saved high-resolution PNG file (Corr_plot.png) current working directory.

title: Check Variable Correlation and Multicollinearity — check
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Example with a data frame df <- data.frame(   temp = rnorm(100, mean = 20, sd = 5),   precip = rnorm(100, mean = 100, sd = 20),   elev = rnorm(100, mean = 500, sd = 100) )  # Add a correlated variable df$temp_correlated <- df$temp * 0.9 + rnorm(100)  result <- check(df)  # Inspect results print(result$vif_table) print(result$correlation.summary) } # }

what: Initialize the Environmental Variable Retrieval Pipeline — var_get
title: Initialize the Environmental Variable Retrieval Pipeline — var_get
text: `var_get()` entry point **envar** package workflow. defines spatial extent, resolution, coordinate reference system (CRS) study area.

title: Initialize the Environmental Variable Retrieval Pipeline — var_get
code: var_get(   country = NULL,   continent = NULL,   shape = NULL,   pointsdf = NULL,   buffer = 0,   res = NULL,   crs = "EPSG:4326" )

title: Initialize the Environmental Variable Retrieval Pipeline — var_get
text: country Character. English name country (e.g., `"Italy"`, `"Viet Nam"`). Used generate extent `shape` NULL. continent Character. English name continent (e.g., `"Europe"`, `"Africa"`). Used generate extent `shape` `country` NULL. shape `sf` object representing study area. can : * **Polygons:** defining region interest. * **Points:** defining specific sampling locations. `shape` provided, `country` `continent` ignored. pointsdf Data.frame columns X Y representing point coordinates. buffer Numeric. buffer distance **kilometers** expand shrink extent. buffer always specified kilometers regardless target CRS - function automatically converts appropriate units internally (degrees geographic CRS like EPSG:4326, meters projected CRS like EPSG:3035 ESRI:54009). * **Positive values**: Expand area outward distance. * **Negative values**: Shrink area inward distance (useful excluding   coastal/border areas data may different characteristics). * **points positive buffer**: circular buffer radius drawn around   point, effectively converting study area polygons. * Default `0`. res Numeric. target spatial resolution multiplier. * controls cell size output raster stack. * Must positive integer (e.g., `1`, `5`, `10`). * Default `1` (30 arc-seconds 0.008333333° equator). * Higher values multiply original 30 arcsec resolution specified factor. crs Character Numeric. Coordinate Reference System **final output**. * Can EPSG code without prefix (e.g., `4326`, `3035`, `EPSG:4326`),   ESRI code (e.g., `54009`, `ESRI:54009`), PROJ4 string, WKT. * `NULL`, pipeline uses standard default WGS84 (EPSG:4326). * specified, downstream environmental layers projected CRS   processing. * Note: ESRI codes (53000-54999, 100000+) automatically recognized prefixed   "ESRI:" internally.

title: Initialize the Environmental Variable Retrieval Pipeline — var_get
text: `list` object (class `envar_par`) containing:   * `grid`: template `SpatRaster` defining resolution extent (polygon input).   * `mask`: `sf` object defining exact study area boundaries (polygon input).   * `res`: resolution multiplier used.   * `bbox`: bounding box study area.   * `crs`: target coordinate reference system.   * `type`: type input ("polygon", "admin", "point").   * `is_global`: Logical, TRUE processing global extent.

title: Initialize the Environmental Variable Retrieval Pipeline — var_get
text: function download data . Instead, creates standardized spatial template (grid) processes point locations passed downstream functions (like `chelsa()`, `worldclim()`, `topography()`, etc.) ensure retrieved variables perfectly aligned stacked. ## works 1. **Extent Definition:** can define study area using shapefile (`sf` object),    `country` name, `continent` name. 2. **Resolution:** `res` argument sets target resolution multiplier    base 30 arc-seconds (~1 km equator). 3. **Buffering:** optional buffer can applied expand study area create    sampling radius around points. buffer **always specified kilometers**,    regardless target CRS. function automatically converts appropriate    units (degrees geographic CRS, meters projected CRS). 4. **Output:**    - input **polygon** (country/continent), returns list containing      target `SpatRaster` grid vector mask.    - input **points** (without buffer), returns point coordinates extraction.    - input **points buffer**, creates polygon geometry around points      returns grid, allowing download raster data area surrounding points.

title: Initialize the Environmental Variable Retrieval Pipeline — var_get
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Basic usage with a country italy_grid <- var_get(country = "Italy")  # With a projected CRS and positive buffer (expand by 50 km) italy_buffered <- var_get(country = "Italy", crs = 3035, buffer = 50)  # With a negative buffer (shrink by 100 km to exclude coastal areas) italy_inland <- var_get(country = "Italy", crs = 54009, buffer = -100)  # Points with buffer to create extraction area points_df <- data.frame(X = c(11.25, 12.5), Y = c(43.77, 41.9)) points_area <- var_get(pointsdf = points_df, buffer = 10, crs = 4326) } # }

text: function downloads, processes, extracts variables WorldClim climate dataset. variable corresponds global raster representing climate variables approximately 1-km resolution. supports Historical (v2.1, 1970-2000) Future (CMIP6) data.

title: Download and process WorldClim Climate Data (Historical & Future) — worldclim
code: worldclim(   x,   vars,   years = NULL,   months = NULL,   gcm = NULL,   rcp = NULL,   ssp = NULL,   ... )

title: Download and process WorldClim Climate Data (Historical & Future) — worldclim
text: x output `var_get()` defining area locations extraction, reference system, buffer. Leave empty use `var_get()` define parameters download. vars Character vector one variables download process. years Character vector years periods. Historical: use "1970-2000" "historical". Future: use "2021-2040", "2041-2060", "2061-2080", "2081-2100". months Numeric vector (1-12) specifying months download. applies monthly historical variables. Ignored bioclimatic variables, elevation, future data. gcm Character vector General Circulation Models (Future data). ssp Character numeric vector Shared Socioeconomic Pathways (e.g., "126", "585"). ... Additional arguments (currently unused).

title: Download and process WorldClim Climate Data (Historical & Future) — worldclim
text: `var_get()` contained raster/polygon/points buffer: `SpatRaster` stack processed variables. `var_get()` contained spatial points data.frame points without buffer: `data.frame` x, y, extracted values.

title: Download and process WorldClim Climate Data (Historical & Future) — worldclim
text: Available variables (working synonyms parentheses): Temperature: 1 - "tmin" ("min temp") 2 - "tmax" ("max temp") 3 - "tavg" ("average temp") Precipitation: 4 - "prec" ("precipitation", "pr") Physical: 5 - "srad" ("solar radiation") 6 - "wind" ("wind speed") 7 - "vapr" ("water vapor") 8 - "elev" ("elevation") Bioclimatic: 9 - "bio" (19 bioclimatic variables), specific e.g., "bio1", "bio12" Citation: Fick, Stephen E., Robert J. Hijmans. "WorldClim 2: new 1-km spatial resolution climate surfaces global land areas." International Journal Climatology 37, . 12 (2017): 4302-4315. https://doi.org/10.1002/joc.5086
